rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component or implementation scope, following AADL structural rules
Preserve existing AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks at the correct scope level
Use visible identifiers from the target component or implementation when available
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented through variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks at the appropriate scope level to maintain architectural integrity
Preserve existing AADL structure when adding AGREE annex blocks
Use visible identifiers from the target component or implementation when available
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented through variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented through variables or outputs
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as typed ports or constants
